ideal_must_detect.matched_text: Confidential Term Sheet — Proposed Share Acquisition of Aoi Data Trust K.K. by Kitanori Holdings Co., Ltd. (Japan)
Transaction overview: Buyer to acquire 100% of Target via negotiated TOB; listing venue: Nippon Securities Exchange (Prime Segment, fictional) — signing targeted Q3 FY2026.

Financing: Mix of senior term loan and privately placed unsecured convertible bonds; no public solicitation; any bond terms not for general distribution under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act.

Cyber/security covenants: Target to deliver third-party SOC2-type report and implement MFA rollout before closing; Buyer reserves JPY 1.2 billion indemnity cap for pre-closing cyber liabilities and any regulatory penalties related to token custody controls.
ESG/climate undertakings: Target to align climate reporting with TCFD-consistent metrics and supply chain data verification; breach of verified ESG KPI may reduce earn-out.
Conduct of business: No new crypto product launches, wallet migrations, or changes to key cloud providers without Buyer consent between signing and closing.
Approvals: Board approvals of both parties required; if external financing is not obtained within exclusivity, Buyer may terminate on payment of a JPY 850 million reverse break fee.
Diligence access limited to de-identified datasets
no Individual Number (My Number) or special-care personal data to be transferred except as permitted by the APPI and with consent or statutory basis
April 2026 cyber intrusion at Target’s cloud archive (currently undisclosed)
insider list to be maintained and updated daily
breach of verified ESG KPI may reduce earn-out
Buyer may terminate on payment of a JPY 850 million reverse break fee

Confidential Term Sheet — Proposed Share Acquisition of Aoi Data Trust K.K. by Kitanori Holdings Co., Ltd. (Japan)
Transaction overview: Buyer to acquire 100% of Target via negotiated TOB; listing venue: Nippon Securities Exchange (Prime Segment, fictional) — signing targeted Q3 FY2026.
Consideration: JPY 42.5 billion cash, plus earn-out up to JPY 5.0 billion linked to verified Scope 3 emissions reduction from Target’s ESG data services over 24 months.
Financing: Mix of senior term loan and privately placed unsecured convertible bonds; no public solicitation; any bond terms not for general distribution under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act.
Key conditions precedent: (i) No-objection letter from the National Financial Services Bureau (fictional) regarding Target’s digital payment token (crypto) custody pilot; (ii) remediation of an April 2026 cyber intrusion at Target’s cloud archive (currently undisclosed).
MNPI handling: Until public announcement under NSE Timely Disclosure Rules (fictional), this term sheet and the cyber and token pilot facts are undisclosed enumerated material facts under the FIEA; selective disclosure and tipping are strictly prohibited.
Information barriers: Buyer will establish a ring-fenced “Project Suisen” deal room; personnel on the Token Markets Desk and climate-credit trading unit to be walled off; insider list to be maintained and updated daily.
Blackout: All employees on the insider list are in a trading blackout for Buyer and Target securities from access date through two full trading days after NSE disclosure (fictional).
Data protection: Diligence access limited to de-identified datasets; no Individual Number (My Number) or special-care personal data to be transferred except as permitted by the APPI and with consent or statutory basis.
Cyber/security covenants: Target to deliver third-party SOC2-type report and implement MFA rollout before closing; Buyer reserves JPY 1.2 billion indemnity cap for pre-closing cyber liabilities and any regulatory penalties related to token custody controls.
ESG/climate undertakings: Target to align climate reporting with TCFD-consistent metrics and supply chain data verification; breach of verified ESG KPI may reduce earn-out.
Conduct of business: No new crypto product launches, wallet migrations, or changes to key cloud providers without Buyer consent between signing and closing.
Employees/retention: Buyer to offer retention grants to key engineers including Mr. Kenjiro Arai and Ms. Mai Fujisawa; any transfer of employee files to comply with APPI and internal privacy rules.
Approvals: Board approvals of both parties required; if external financing is not obtained within exclusivity, Buyer may terminate on payment of a JPY 850 million reverse break fee.

must_detect.matched_text:
Proposed Share Acquisition of Aoi Data Trust K.K. by Kitanori Holdings Co., Ltd. (Japan)
Transaction overview: Buyer to acquire 100% of Target via negotiated TOB; listing venue: Nippon Securities Exchange (Prime Segment, fictional) — signing targeted Q3 FY2026.
Consideration: JPY 42.5 billion cash, plus earn-out up to JPY 5.0 billion linked to verified Scope 3 emissions reduction from Target’s ESG data services over 24 months.
Financing: Mix of senior term loan and privately placed unsecured convertible bonds; no public solicitation; any bond terms not for general distribution under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act.
Key conditions precedent: (i) No-objection letter from the National Financial Services Bureau (fictional) regarding Target’s digital payment token (crypto) custody pilot; (ii) remediation of an April 2026 cyber intrusion at Target’s cloud archive (currently undisclosed).
MNPI handling: Until public announcement under NSE Timely Disclosure Rules (fictional), this term sheet and the cyber and token pilot facts are undisclosed enumerated material facts under the FIEA; selective disclosure and tipping are strictly prohibited.
Information barriers: Buyer will establish a ring-fenced “Project Suisen” deal room; personnel on the Token Markets Desk and climate-credit trading unit to be walled off; insider list to be maintained and updated daily.
Blackout: All employees on the insider list are in a trading blackout for Buyer and Target securities from access date through two full trading days after NSE disclosure (fictional).
Data protection: Diligence access limited to de-identified datasets; no Individual Number (My Number) or special-care personal data to be transferred except as permitted by the APPI and with consent or statutory basis.
Cyber/security covenants: Target to deliver third-party SOC2-type report and implement MFA rollout before closing; Buyer reserves JPY 1.2 billion indemnity cap for pre-closing cyber liabilities and any regulatory penalties related to token custody controls.
ESG/climate undertakings: Target to align climate reporting with TCFD-consistent metrics and supply chain data verification; breach of verified ESG KPI may reduce earn-out.
Conduct of business: No new crypto product launches, wallet migrations, or changes to key cloud providers without Buyer consent between signing and closing.
Employees/retention: Buyer to offer retention grants to key engineers including Mr. Kenjiro Arai and Ms. Mai Fujisawa; any transfer of employee files to comply with APPI and internal privacy rules.
Approvals: Board approvals of both parties required; if external financing is not obtained within exclusivity, Buyer may terminate on payment of a JPY 850 million reverse break fee.
Confidentiality and communications: All media responses coordinated by Buyer’s IR; public release to be via NSE disclosure (fictional) and corporate website; all inquiries to Ms. Rika Nogawa, General Counsel, rika.nogawa@kitanori.example
